description: Cross-domain citation rate remeasurement at N=901 (S430 resolution test)
method: generalizer_expert.py cross-domain rate (pre-registered metric). N = total lessons; cross_domain = lessons with citations spanning 2+ domains in Cites: header.
expect: Cross-domain citation rate 6.0-6.5% at N=898 (natural growth from 5.87% at N=875).
actual: 5.66% (51/901). BELOW target. Denominator grew +26 lessons, cross-domain count unchanged at 51. No organic cross-domain lessons in S427-S430 era.
diff: Expected natural growth to 6%+. Actual: rate DECLINED from 5.87% to 5.66% — dilution effect. New lessons in S427-S430 era are domain-specific DOMEX work with zero cross-domain citations. Cross-domain production rate = 0/26 = 0% in this window.

resolution_gap.projected_sessions: At current 0% cross-domain production rate, never. At historical 3% baseline, ~133 more lessons (15-20 sessions).

diagnosis: DOMEX dispatch creates domain-focused sessions by design. Cross-domain citations only arise from: (1) historian synthesis sessions, (2) generalizer sprint sessions, (3) organic multi-domain experiments. None of these occurred S427-S430. F-EXP8 target of 6% requires explicit cross-domain production, not organic growth.
f_exp8_status: OPEN — 5.66% vs 6.0% target. Rate declining due to dilution. Resolution requires either: (a) 4 cross-domain lessons via targeted generalizer sessions, or (b) lowering target to 5.5% as a natural ceiling for dispatch-driven production.
meta_note: The Goodhart pattern: dispatch optimizes domain depth, which mechanically dilutes cross-domain breadth. Same tension as L-927 (UCB1 yield vs Gini diversity).
